ev.headline: Uber Invests $1.25 Billion in Rivian for 50,000 Robotaxis Partnership
ev.summary: Uber announced a major investment in Rivian to deploy up to 50,000 all-electric robotaxis starting with the new R2 SUV, with an initial $300 million investment and options for 40,000 more vehicles from 2030.

ev.headline: Xiaomi Launches Next-Generation SU7 Electric Sedan with 902 km Range
ev.summary: Xiaomi officially launched the next-generation SU7 with sweeping upgrades including up to 902 km CLTC range, standard LiDAR across all trims, and 897V charging architecture on the top model.

ev.headline: Stellantis EVs Gain Access to Tesla Supercharger Network
ev.summary: Jeep, Dodge and Stellantis EVs can now plug in and charge on Tesla's Supercharger network, the largest DC charging network in North America, completing access for nearly all EVs in America.

ev.headline: Lexus Launches 2026 ES EV with 300+ Miles Range Under $50,000
ev.summary: Lexus launched the 2026 ES EV luxury electric sedan with over 300 miles of range and prices starting under $50,000, offering affordability in the luxury EV segment.

ev.headline: BMW Launches First All-Electric 3 Series with 800V Ultra-Fast Charging
ev.summary: The first all-electric BMW 3 Series debuted featuring 800-volt architecture and ultra-fast charging speeds, representing a major milestone for the luxury automaker's electrification.

ev.headline: BYD's Flash Chargers Deliver 1,500 Kilowatts of Power to Single EV
ev.summary: BYD introduced new Flash Chargers capable of delivering up to 1,500 kilowatts of power to a single EV, advancing ultra-fast charging infrastructure capabilities.

ev.headline: GM Takes $7.6 Billion Charge in January 2026, $6B Tied to Ultium Platform
ev.summary: General Motors took $7.6 billion in charges in January 2026, with $6 billion directly tied to its Ultium EV platform write-down, marking the largest EV-related strategic write-down in automotive history.

ev.headline: Ford Takes $19.5 Billion Charge for EV Pullback and Restructuring
ev.summary: Ford Motor announced a $19.5 billion charge related to restructuring and pullback in all-electric vehicle investments, pivoting toward hybrid and ICE vehicles while maintaining smaller profitable EV models.

ev.headline: U.S. EV Sales Collapse 41% Amid Federal Incentive Expiration
ev.summary: U.S. EV sales plummeted from 10.3% market share in September to 5.2% in Q4 2025 following the expiration of federal tax credits, marking a significant demand decline.

ev.headline: Honda Cancels Three EVs, Refocuses Strategy on Hybrids
ev.summary: Honda canceled three electric vehicles including the 0 Series SUV as it refocuses on hybrid development amid market uncertainty and policy changes.

ev.headline: Volkswagen ID. Cross Subcompact EV Launching at €28,000 in Fall
ev.summary: Volkswagen's new €28,000 subcompact electric crossover, the ID. Cross, is coming in the fall targeting mainstream European buyers with an affordable price point.

ev.headline: Mercedes-Benz Introduces All-Electric VLE Grand Limousine with 800V Charging
ev.summary: Mercedes-Benz revealed the all-electric VLE, an eight-seat grand limousine featuring 800-volt charging, over 700 km estimated WLTP range, and flexible interior on new Van Architecture.

ev.headline: Dacia Reveals 2030 Roadmap with Four Electric Vehicles and 67% Electrification Target
ev.summary: Dacia unveiled its 2030 roadmap featuring four electric vehicles and the new Striker crossover, targeting electrification of two-thirds of sales through hybrids and EVs.

ev.headline: Porsche Adds Cayenne S Electric to All-Electric SUV Lineup
ev.summary: Porsche expanded its all-electric SUV lineup with the new Cayenne S Electric featuring 544 PS, 653 km range, and direct oil cooling on Turbo-exclusive chassis options.

ev.headline: Lead the Charge 2026 Report Shows Automakers Advancing Supply Chain Decarbonization
ev.summary: The Lead the Charge Leaderboard ranked global automakers with Ford, Mercedes, Tesla, Volvo and Volkswagen leading efforts to build sustainable, fossil-free EV supply chains.

ev.headline: California Accelerates ZEV Sales Target to 100% by 2035 Under New ACC II Rule
ev.summary: California's Advanced Clean Cars II regulation requires 35% ZEV sales in 2026, 68% by 2030, and 100% by 2035 with new battery warranty requirements.

ev.headline: Trump Administration Rolls Back Federal EV Tax Credits and CAFE Standards
ev.summary: The Trump administration eliminated federal EV purchase tax credits by September 2025 and proposed rolling back fuel economy standards, significantly impacting EV market momentum.

ev.headline: Ford Converts Tennessee Plant from EV to Gas Truck Production, Hires 1,000 Workers
ev.summary: Ford's Tennessee factory, originally built for EV production, was converted to gas-powered truck manufacturing with 1,000 new hires to boost conventional F-150 and Super Duty output.

ev.headline: Automakers Shift Strategy Toward Hybrids as EV Pivot Continues in 2026
ev.summary: U.S. automakers are rapidly expanding hybrid offerings and shifting production priorities away from all-electric vehicles, with CAFE standard rollbacks enabling this transition.

ev.headline: EV Manufacturing Gets Easier and Cheaper, Survey Shows Higher Output Expected in 2026
ev.summary: A survey of 473 automotive decision-makers found EV manufacturing is getting easier and cheaper, with most producers expecting higher output in 2026.

ev.headline: Renault's futuREady Plan Targets Two Million Sales by 2030 with 800V Platform
ev.summary: Renault unveiled its futuREady plan targeting two million vehicle sales by 2030, anchored by new 800V electric platform, extended hybrid commitment, and expansion across five international hubs.

ev.headline: PwC Projects EV Market to Recover to 19% by 2030 Despite 2026 Headwinds
ev.summary: PwC forecasts that despite current EV adoption slowdown, the market will pick up toward end of decade with EVs reaching 19% of U.S. industry by 2030.

ev.headline: Rivian R2 Pricing Near $60,000 at Launch; Base Trim Coming Late 2027
ev.summary: Rivian priced its R2 crossover near $60,000 at launch as a Tesla Model Y rival, with the base trim arriving in late 2027.

ev.headline: Scout Motors Shows 87% of Reservation Holders Prefer Range-Extended Over Full EV
ev.summary: Scout Motors revealed that 87% of its 160,000 reservation holders chose the range-extended version over full electric, signaling consumer preference for flexibility.

ev.headline: Hyundai Launches Casper Electric Lounge as Most Affordable EV Option
ev.summary: Hyundai launched the Casper Electric Lounge as the top-tier trim featuring luxurious interior, representing the company's most affordable electric vehicle offering.

energy.headline: California court upholds NEM 3.0 net metering cuts, discouraging rooftop solar expansion
energy.summary: A California appeals court ruled in favor of utility regulators who slashed net metering payments by 75%, upholding NEM 3.0 policy changes that significantly reduce compensation for rooftop solar exports to the grid.

energy.headline: UK accelerates solar deployment amid energy security concerns with Warm Homes Plan support
energy.summary: The UK government is accelerating residential solar expansion and battery storage adoption through grants and interest-free loans as part of its energy security response to Middle East-related price pressures.

energy.headline: Solar-plus-storage projects advance across US with increased residential battery adoption
energy.summary: Residential battery storage grew 51% in 2025 with projects expanding from Texas to Michigan, driven by changing net metering rules and utility partnerships for grid services and virtual power plants.

energy.headline: Colorado clears way for affordable plug-in solar and meter collars to lower electrification costs
energy.summary: Colorado legislators backing House Bill 1007 are enabling portable 'plug-in' solar panels for about $2,000 and meter collars for $500-700, bypassing expensive electrical panel upgrades needed for rooftop systems.

energy.headline: Batteries becoming dominant strategy as net metering changes and energy prices surge nationwide
energy.summary: Home battery storage is increasingly replacing pure solar strategies as changing net metering policies reduce grid export compensation and time-of-use rates incentivize self-consumption.

energy.headline: U.S. residential solar market enters post-incentive era after 205% installation surge before tax credit expiration
energy.summary: Homeowners rushed to install solar systems in late 2025 before federal tax credits expired, creating a 205% surge in installations and supply constraints that test market resilience.

energy.headline: 24.3 GW of new battery storage to come online in 2026, marking expansion of solar-plus-storage systems
energy.summary: The EIA projects record battery energy storage deployments in 2026, with solar and storage accounting for 79% of all planned utility-scale capacity additions as residential battery attachment rates surge.

energy.headline: Residential storage grew 51% in 2025 as homeowners defer solar investments for later battery retrofits
energy.summary: Benchmark data shows residential battery installations surged 51% in 2025, with homeowners prioritizing solar systems while planning future battery additions as grid-services opportunities expand.

energy.headline: One in eight American homes projected to have solar by 2030 with vast majority pairing storage
energy.summary: Analysts project dramatic solar adoption growth with 23% of California's residential battery attachment rate expected to become national norm, driven by net metering policy changes and time-of-use rates.

energy.headline: Massachusetts maintains strong net metering support with expanded 10+ kW eligibility for residential solar
energy.summary: Massachusetts offers one of the strongest net metering policies with full retail-rate credits and recently expanded eligibility thresholds, making residential solar economically attractive versus other states.

energy.headline: California solar incentives update: SGIP budgets exhausted, net billing remains primary compensation
energy.summary: As of March 2026, California's ratepayer-funded SGIP battery incentive budgets are completely closed, leaving property tax exemptions and utility programs as primary incentives for new installations.

energy.headline: New York offers 25% state solar tax credit up to $5,000 with stackable federal incentives through 2026
energy.summary: New York homeowners can claim 25% of system costs (up to $5,000) via state tax credit while federal incentives remain available, with NY-Sun rebates and property tax exemptions also offered.

energy.headline: Arizona moves to net billing, eliminating full retail net metering for new residential solar systems
energy.summary: Arizona utilities including APS, SRP, and TEP no longer offer traditional full retail net metering for new systems, instead providing fixed export rate credits that incentivize pairing solar with batteries.

energy.headline: Washington state offers sales tax exemptions and PSE net metering, with possible program changes looming
energy.summary: Washington provides ongoing sales tax exemptions through 2029 and net metering credits through PSE, but experts warn the program may change soon, creating urgency for 2026 installations.

energy.headline: Battery pack costs hit $108/kWh in 2026, enabling multi-day backup and grid services capabilities
energy.summary: Bloomberg NEF reports battery costs declining to $108/kWh with further 3% reductions expected in 2026, making multi-day backup and virtual power plant participation economically viable.

energy.headline: Virtual power plants dispatched 535 MW from 100,000+ California homes during demand peaks
energy.summary: Sunrun's virtual power plant test in California demonstrated the capability to dispatch 535 megawatts from residential solar and storage systems, showing potential for distributed energy management.

energy.headline: Puerto Rico's residential solar reaches 175,000 homes with 160,000 paired with battery storage
energy.summary: Puerto Rico achieved a solar revolution with 175,000 homes now hosting residential solar and 160,000 systems including battery storage, enabling grid stability during summer demand peaks.

energy.headline: LG Energy Solution secures $4.3 billion Tesla battery supply agreement announced by US government
energy.summary: LG Energy Solution reached a major supply deal with Tesla for battery cells while simultaneously upgrading EV battery production capacity with General Motors for energy storage systems.

energy.headline: Texas renewables and storage boom improves grid resilience, better prepared than 2021 winter
energy.summary: Texas experienced significant renewable energy and battery storage expansion since Winter Storm Uri, improving the grid's ability to handle extreme weather while reducing fossil fuel reliance.

energy.headline: DIY solar legislation introduced in more than half of U.S. states to boost residential adoption
energy.summary: Over half of U.S. states have introduced bipartisan legislation supporting DIY solar systems, lowering barriers to residential installations as manufacturing and installation costs decline.

energy.headline: Colorado home battery storage emerging as most exciting clean energy area amid tariff and incentive changes
energy.summary: Solar installation experts identify plug-in solar with battery storage as the most promising clean energy sector, especially as federal tax credits expire and tariff costs rise for larger systems.

energy.headline: North Carolina nonprofit rooftop solar inspires nearby Goodwill headquarters to install system
energy.summary: A North Carolina nonprofit's rooftop solar installation inspired neighboring Goodwill headquarters to install its own panels, planning to redirect energy bill savings back to job training programs.

energy.headline: New York FranklinWH battery storage program offers $3,000 incentive and ~$200 annual earnings
energy.summary: New York homeowners can receive $3,000 upfront incentives for FranklinWH battery storage systems and earn approximately $200 annually through National Grid and Orange & Rockland grid service programs.

energy.headline: Base Power raises $100 million to install 100 MW residential storage outside Dallas faster than gas plants
energy.summary: Home battery startup Base Power will deploy 100 megawatts of residential energy storage systems outside Dallas faster than building equivalent fossil fuel generation capacity.

energy.headline: Post-incentive solar pricing reflects market competition with 24% pricing spread as installer capacity fills
energy.summary: Despite capacity constraints in late 2025, solar pricing remained stable with median costs at $2.50/W, though the spread between highest and lowest quotes expanded to a record $0.72/W.

v2x.headline: Study Details V2G Adoption Barriers in US - Standardization and Interoperability Emerge as Key Challenges
v2x.summary: New NC State research examines barriers to vehicle-to-grid adoption through interviews with 42 stakeholders, finding that standardization, interoperability, and infrastructure readiness present the most salient obstacles to widespread deployment.

v2x.headline: GM Expands Vehicle-to-Home Technology Across 12 EV Models
v2x.summary: General Motors now supports bidirectional V2H charging across 12 models spanning Chevrolet, GMC, and Cadillac brands, representing a major shift in EV capabilities and market availability.

v2x.headline: Xos Announces V2G Production Beginning April 2026 for Commercial EV Fleet
v2x.summary: Commercial EV builder Xos launches V2G production on electric school buses this April, enabling fleet operators to generate revenue by discharging stored energy during peak demand without hardware retrofits.

v2x.headline: Massachusetts Deploys 100 Bidirectional EV Chargers in First Statewide V2X Pilot
v2x.summary: Massachusetts Clean Energy Center will deploy 100 free bidirectional chargers at no cost to residential, commercial, and school customers, adding 1.5 MW of distributed energy storage by September 2026.

v2x.headline: Nissan Announces Affordable Bidirectional Charging Launch in 2026
v2x.summary: Nissan plans to launch affordable bidirectional charging on select EVs in 2026, with AC-based systems priced comparable to traditional unidirectional chargers for broader market accessibility.

v2x.headline: Toyota Advances V2G Pilot with Oncor Energy at Plano Headquarters
v2x.summary: Toyota demonstrates real-world V2G value through pilot program with Texas utility Oncor, studying grid stability enhancement and infrastructure longevity improvements through bidirectional BEV charging.

v2x.headline: Wallbox and Bidirectional Energy Secure $2.2M California Grant for V2X Chargers
v2x.summary: Partnership receives CEC funding to deploy residential V2X demonstration project with Wallbox Quasar 2 bidirectional chargers, enabling V2H and V2G capabilities for California homeowners.

v2x.headline: Kia EV9 Owners Access Vehicle-to-Home Capabilities with Wallbox Quasar 2
v2x.summary: Wallbox launches Quasar 2 bidirectional charger for Kia EV9 owners, offering up to 12 kW V2H power at $6,440 retail, enabling home backup power and energy cost savings.

v2x.headline: UC Irvine Launches Mass-Market V2H Deployment with Kia EV9 and Wallbox Quasar 2
v2x.summary: Six Menifee, California homes begin two-year evaluation of Wallbox Quasar 2 bidirectional chargers paired with Kia EV9 vehicles, demonstrating V2H technology at residential scale.

v2x.headline: CALSTART Report: EV Battery Grid Integration and Top Trends for 2026
v2x.summary: CALSTART identifies top 10 EV battery trends including ultra-fast charging, LFP battery growth, and potential V2G integration with machine-learning systems for enhanced grid efficiency.

v2x.headline: Essential Energy Launches Market-Ready V2G Technology in Australia
v2x.summary: Essential Energy unveils market-ready vehicle-to-grid charging technology in Australia in April 2025, unlocking customer value from renewable energy investments.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV Charger Market Forecast: $4.1 Billion by 2036 at 14.8% CAGR
v2x.summary: V2X bidirectional charger market expected to grow from current levels to $4.1 billion by 2036, with US and UK leading growth through federal incentives and smart grid mandates.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Grid Market Projected to Reach $34.84 Billion by 2035
v2x.summary: Global V2G market expected to grow from $4.28 billion in 2025 to $34.84 billion by 2035 at 23.32% CAGR, with hardware components holding 67.78% market share.

v2x.headline: Nissan and ChargeScape Launch V2G Pilot in Silicon Valley
v2x.summary: Nissan and ChargeScape initiate vehicle-to-grid pilot in Silicon Valley Power territory to address growing grid demand from AI data centers using California Energy Commission backing.

v2x.headline: Ford F-150 Lightning Powers Veterinary Clinic During Hurricane Helene
v2x.summary: Emergency veterinary clinic in Florida used Ford F-150 Lightning's V2L/V2H bidirectional charging to power surgical equipment during Hurricane Helene, demonstrating real-world emergency resilience.

v2x.headline: Mercedes-Benz Announces Bidirectional Charging for 2026 with New GLC EV
v2x.summary: Mercedes-Benz confirmed introduction of bidirectional charging for 2026, with the all-electric GLC leading the way alongside MB.CHARGE Home ecosystem integration.

v2x.headline: Oakland School District Becomes First in US to Deploy 100% Electric Buses with V2G
v2x.summary: Oakland Unified School District transitions to fully electric school bus system with vehicle-to-grid technology, marking first statewide US achievement of this scale.

v2x.headline: Nissan LEAF Approved for Enhanced Bidirectional Charger from Fermata Energy
v2x.summary: Nissan approved Fermata Energy's FE-20 enhanced bidirectional charger for Nissan LEAF use, positioning the LEAF as the only fully electric passenger vehicle in US able to supply energy to grid.

v2x.headline: 2026 Marks Breakthrough Year for Bidirectional EV Charging Mainstream Adoption
v2x.summary: EcoFlow analysis indicates 2026 is tipping point for bidirectional charging as standard feature, with majority of new EVs providing V2L capability while V2G/V2H limited to specific manufacturers.

v2x.headline: Sunrun and BGE Launch First US Residential V2G Power Plant with Ford F-150 Lightning
v2x.summary: Solar and storage provider Sunrun partners with Maryland utility BGE to operate first residential vehicle-to-grid distributed power plant using Ford F-150 Lightning trucks.

v2x.headline: V2G Market Set to Hit $5.8 Billion by 2036 - 2026 Marks Takeoff Point
v2x.summary: Torque News analysis forecasts V2G technology market reaching $5.8 billion by 2036, with 2026 marking critical inflection point as EV expansion drives grid modernization shift.

v2x.headline: Porsche Tests Swarm V2G Technology to Stabilize Grid with EV Batteries
v2x.summary: Porsche runs pilot program pooling high-voltage batteries from multiple EVs to demonstrate swarm V2G capabilities for stabilizing local electrical grids.

v2x.headline: Hyundai IONIQ 5 Deployed in World's First Bidirectional Region - Utrecht, Netherlands
v2x.summary: Hyundai partners with We Drive Solar in Utrecht to create world's first bidirectional region using fleet of IONIQ 5 vehicles with V2G technology for innovative mobility service.
